itemGroups.insight: Rush <ScItemInline itemName="Hextech Rocketbelt" /> for the active dash, ability haste, and AP. Follow up with <ScItemInline itemName="Sorcerer's Shoes" /> for magic pen, then <ScItemInline itemName="Shadowflame" /> for even more pen and crit damage.
itemGroups.title: Core Build
Fourth Item Options
itemGroups.insightHeader: For my Next Trick, your HP will Vanish
itemGroups.insight: if enemies aren't stacking MR. Into 4+ squishy comps, you can also go \n\nPick up if they're stacking MR instead.

itemGroups.title: Fifth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: Buy <ScItemInline itemName="Rabadon's Deathcap" /> if you went <ScItemInline itemName="Stormsurge" /> or <ScItemInline itemName="Void Staff" /> 4th. Or buy <ScItemInline itemName="Void Staff" /> if you went <ScItemInline itemName="Rabadon's Deathcap" /> 4th.

itemGroups.title: Sixth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: Buy <ScItemInline itemName="Stormsurge" /> if you don't have it yet and don't need the next 2 options. Go <ScItemInline itemName="Banshee's Veil" /> if the enemy has strong disengage or AP poke. Pick up <ScItemInline itemName="Zhonya's Hourglass" /> if the enemy AD threat is too strong.

insights.title: E to End the Trade
insights.reason:
Aurora's <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> is one of the most useful parts of her kit. It has EVERYTHING - a slow, damage, and a very tiny dash that sends you backwards after casting, which is perfect for kiting (although not the best for chasing).

* Your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> can actually take you ==through== ==walls.== But **ONLY** the tiniest walls - like the one near the right side of top tri-bush, or the entrance to blue-side jungle. 
* Because the ability takes you backwards, it is best used at the ==END== ==of== ==a== ==trade== rather than at the start. Much like lvl 1, your combo is <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" /> > auto as many times as you can > <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> when you're ready to disengage > <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" />.
* Use it when your opponent is about to leave your range. Since they're going to be far away anyways, casting <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> is just free damage.

insights.title: Choice to Make at 3

insights.reason:
<ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" /> is one of the most broken abilities in the game. It grants you invisiblity for 1-1.6 seconds depending on rank, but also a TON of movespeed for 4 whole seconds. This is what lets you kite out enemy top laners, even if they have access to a dash.

* Make sure to use the movespeed duration to reposition, whether that's chasing or kiting out an enemy that jumped on you. Unless you can quickly kill your opponent, it's not really worth autoing because you'll be using up your precious 4 seconds of movespeed time being stationary locked in auto-animation as opposed to spacing.
* In teamfights, your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" possessive />'s cooldown is reset after every takedown. This is what makes Aurora so slippery and unkillable - use your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" /> to reposition aggressively or kite in fights.
insights.title: USE YOUR MS

electrocute-sudden-impact.insight:
Aurora has one of the ==highest== ==kill== ==potentials== of ranged tops thanks to her high base damage and ability to reliably proc Domination runes.

* Proc <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Electrocute" /> with <ScComboInline steps={["Q", "AA", "Q2"]} />.
* Proc <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Sudden Impact" /> with just <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> > <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Scorch" />, or <ScComboInline steps={["W", "Q", "AA"]} />.
* **Her level 1 & 2 trade pattern is** **extremely oppressive** - even if your opponents jump on you, you'll outtrade them with your Q + running them down with autos as they run away.

manaflow-scorch.insight: Aurora is one of the best lane bullies in top lane. The only thing she lacks in top is consistency in landing her <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" />, but this is easier in top since most matchups are melees that need to lasthit in front of you. If you land your abilities, you will 100% win the trade.\n* Take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Manaflow Band" /> for mana sustain throughout the game - your mana costs are so low that you don't even need to build mana items, and <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Manaflow Band" /> is all you need.\n* Take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Scorch" /> to maximize your snowball potential. You want to get your opponents low to benefit from <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" /> execute damage, and <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Scorch" /> can proc <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Sudden Impact" /> on your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" />!

grisly-mementos-ultimate-hunter.insight:
Aurora doesn't build much ability haste she prefers raw AP and magic penetration when possible.

* Take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Ultimate Hunter" /> to increase your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="R" /> uptime.
* Take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Grisly Mementos" /> to lower the cooldown of <ScSummonerInline summonerIdentifier="Oracle Lens" />, which is crucial for ==securing== ==flanks== in team-fights.

runesv3.summonerSpellExplanation: You can take <ScSummonerInline summonerIdentifier="Teleport" /> versus tanks like <ScChampionInline championId="KSante" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Sion" /> that you don't think you can kill.
